source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A dog is tied to the outside corner of a house by a rope of length $10 \mathrm{~m}$. The house is a rectangle with sides of length $6 \mathrm{~m}$ and $4 \mathrm{~m}$. What is the length (in metres) of the curved boundary of the area in which the dog can roam? <image1>

image_dependency: question text fully specifies the geometry: rope length and rectangle side lengths are given explicitly
an image exists in the registry, but the wording does not explicitly require reading visual details from it
multi_step: solving requires geometric reasoning about how the reachable boundary changes around a rectangular corner
the problem combines multiple quantities from the text (rope length 10 m; house sides 6 m and 4 m)
the target is not a direct lookup; it requires deriving the curved boundary length from the configuration
verifiability: answer is a concise symbolic expression: $20\pi$
multiple-choice options are provided, enabling straightforward answer checking

decision_reasons: 资产完整：题干、答案和图片均存在，且 registry_passed=true、issues 为空
题目核心语义可由文本充分支撑，图像依赖低，不存在因资产缺失导致不可恢复的问题
综合质量较好：multi_step_score=0.68，具备一定推理深度
可验证性高：verifiability_score=0.93，答案为简洁表达式且便于核验
未见风险标记，适合进入候选池
